response.justification: Szabo is a skilled, versatile engineer with 20+ years in the industry and valuable expertise in distributed systems and event-driven architectures. However, he lacks critical CTO-level requirements: no demonstrated fintech domain knowledge (credit systems, trading, risk, compliance), no clear production multi-agent AI shipping at scale, no track record scaling engineering teams to 30+ people or developing senior technical leadership, and no regulated-environment experience. His current agentic work at re:cinq is promising but underdeveloped on the resume. He is a strong principal engineer or technical architect, not an executive leader for this fintech-AI mandate.
strengths: 20+ years of engineering with deep distributed-systems expertise (Dunnhumby microservices, high-throughput systems, real-time databases)
Currently working on agentic AI infrastructure, MCP frameworks, and event-driven systems at re:cinq, showing direct exposure to emerging AI-orchestration tooling
Full-stack technical depth across backend, DevOps, and infrastructure; track record of architectural thinking and building open-source platforms
concerns: No fintech background or domain knowledge—no evidence of credit systems, payment flows, trading, risk assessment, or regulatory compliance work
Resume does not demonstrate executive team leadership scaling from 10 to 30+ people or development of senior technical leadership layers, critical for CTO role
Agentic AI work is recent and vaguely described; no demonstrated production multi-agent systems at scale, no LLM selection/evaluation experience, and no regulated-environment AI operations

key_factors.factor: No executive team scaling or tech leadership layer development
